DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendants THE 3045 JACKSON CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGMENT, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Joseph Tomasik, Esq., Law Offices of, (510) 848-0500, for Plaintiff Nora Batha.

Christian L. Woods, Esq., CLINTON & CLINTON, (562) 216-5000, for Defendants,
THE 3045 JACKSON CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION and HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.

At the outset, counsel for Defendants advises that he submits to the tentative rulings however, requests twenty (20) days to file a responsive pleading in open court. No objection to the request is provided. The court advises that it will include Defendants request in the proposed order.

The court adopts the following tentative ruling:

Defendants THE 3045 JACKSON CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGMENT, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is overruled. The complaint is not uncertain; rather it is quite detailed and informative. The complaint adequately pleads the elements of an Unruh Act claim. (CACI 3060.) Plaintiff alleges she is disabled and defendants denied her full and equal access to public space in their condominium building by a series of unsafe and/or illegal conditions. (See, e.g., Cmplt. at 1:21-4:23, 6:28-18:8, 22:11-22, 27:22-23.) Where ADA-mandated access is at issue in an Unruh Act case, intentional discrimination is not required. (Munson v. Del Taco, Inc. (2009) 46 Cal.4th 661, 678.) The complaint adequately pleads a California Disabled Persons Act claim. As already found, proximate cause is pled. Further, the complaint alleges (see above) that plaintiff was denied "full and equal access" to public space in defendants' condominium building on a particular occasion on the basis of her disability. (Civ. Code 54.1; see also Donald v. Cafe Royale, Inc. (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 168 [as here, plaintiff allegedly denied full and equal access to public space].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***After Hearing: An order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendants THE 3045 JACKSON CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGMENT, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Joseph Tomasik, Esq., Law Offices of, (510) 848-0500, for Plaintiff Nora Batha.

Christian L. Woods, Esq., CLINTON & CLINTON, (562) 216-5000, for Defendants,
THE 3045 JACKSON CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION and HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.

At the outset, counsel for Defendants advises that he submits to the tentative rulings however, requests twenty (20) days to file a responsive pleading in open court. No objection to the request is provided. The court advises that it will include Defendants request in the proposed order.

The court adopts the following tentative ruling: Defendants THE 3045 JACKSON CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGMENT, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. At the pleading stage, bases for punitive damages are adequately alleged. (See, e.g., Cmplt. 21:8-22:7, 22:11-12, 27:22-23.) Corporate employees are not an issue in this case. Statutory damages and attorney fees are available under plaintiff's statutory claims at a minimum.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***After Hearing: An order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
